name: Collect and Sort
summary: Dump all values from both lists into one array, sort it, and return.
visualizationIntent: init: show combined array being built element by element. loop: current element highlighted as it is appended. update: combined array grows visibly after each append. return: freeze on the final sorted result.

lineExplanations.3: Create an empty list that will hold all values from both inputs.
lineExplanations.4: Iterate over every element in the first sorted list.
lineExplanations.5: Add the current element from list1 into the combined array.
lineExplanations.6: Iterate over every element in the second sorted list.
lineExplanations.7: Add the current element from list2 into the combined array.
lineExplanations.8: Sort the combined array in-place — this is the O((n+m) log(n+m)) step.
lineExplanations.9: Assign the sorted array to result so the visualizer can display it.

syntaxExplanations.1: class Solution: a wrapper class required by LeetCode and Knacktor.
syntaxExplanations.2: def mergeTwoLists(self, list1, list2): declares the method with two input lists.
syntaxExplanations.3: combined = [] creates a new empty list literal.
syntaxExplanations.4: for v in list1: iterates over each element v in list1 one at a time.
syntaxExplanations.5: combined.append(v) adds element v to the end of the combined list.
syntaxExplanations.6: for v in list2: iterates over each element v in list2 one at a time.
syntaxExplanations.7: combined.append(v) adds element v from list2 to the end of combined.
syntaxExplanations.8: combined.sort() sorts the list in-place (modifies combined directly, returns None).
syntaxExplanations.9: result = combined gives the sorted list a name the visualizer tracks.
syntaxExplanations.10: return result sends the merged sorted list back to the caller.
